name: IRC_GV_MASK_FIELDS
description: This table is created to save the information of the fields to be masked.

columns.Name: MASK_FIELD_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key for this table. System generated.

columns.Name: GV_CONTEXT_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the context code for which all the views will be stored. For example : Context can be ORA_SUBMISSION and all the fields related to Job Application would be specified with this context code.

columns.Name: FIELD_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the code for the field. Field Code can be defined like this ORA_*CONTEXT*_FIELD. For example - Candidate Name Field for Job Application Context , the field code can be ORA_JA_CAND_NAME

columns.Name: FIELD_TYPE_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the information about what type of field is being defined. For eg :- ORA_GENERAL , ORA_COMPOUND , ORA_EFF_GENERAL , ORA_EFF_COMPOUND

columns.Name: FLEX_SEGMENT_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the segment code of the flex field.

columns.Name: FLEX_CONTEXT_CODE
columns.Comments: Stores the context code of the flex field.

columns.Name: PROFILE_CONTENT_SECTION_ID
columns.Comments: This column stores the Section Id for a given category.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
